title: LLM-as-a-Judge for Tasks
concept: Using a separate evaluation model to inspect execution logs and outputs, scoring task completeness.

exercises.prompt: What is a key aspect of measuring success in tasks?
exercises.explain: Defining success criteria is essential to measure how well a task was completed.

cards.title: Introducing LLM-as-a-Judge
cards.body: An LLM (Large Language Model) can evaluate how well tasks are performed by analyzing execution logs and outputs.

cards.title: What is Evaluation?
cards.body: Evaluation refers to the process of examining task performance to determine if it meets certain success criteria.

cards.title: LLM as a Detective
cards.body: Think of the LLM as a detective reviewing evidence. Just as detectives inspect clues, the LLM inspects logs to score how well tasks were completed.

cards.title: Example of Evaluation
cards.body: After a task is performed, the LLM checks the outputs against the success criteria to assign a score for completeness.

cards.title: Myth: Evaluation is Unnecessary
cards.body: Some may think evaluation isn't needed, but it's crucial to understand how well tasks are done and where to improve.

cards.title: Comparing With and Without Evaluation
cards.body: Evaluating tasks helps ensure better outcomes.

right.text: Clear understanding of success and areas for improvement.

exercises.prompt: What does an LLM do during evaluation?
exercises.explain: The LLM scores task completeness by analyzing execution logs and determining if the output meets the success criteria.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The LLM only creates new tasks and doesn't evaluate them.
exercises.explain: False. The LLM is also responsible for evaluating how well tasks were performed.

exercises.prompt: The LLM inspects ___ to score task completeness.
exercises.sentence: The LLM inspects execution logs to score task completeness.
exercises.explain: The LLM inspects execution logs for details on how tasks were carried out and to measure their success.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following shows how an LLM evaluates a task?

options.text: It checks if tasks are finished
It decides if tasks were fun

exercises.explain: The LLM evaluates a task by analyzing the responses and logs to determine how well it was completed.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important for an LLM to evaluate tasks?

options.text: To improve task performance over time

exercises.explain: Evaluating tasks is important because it helps identify performance levels and improve how tasks are completed in future instances.
